project.name: fjp-workflow-dashboard
project.description: Zero-backend DokoKin/FJP kintai (attendance) automation system: a vanilla-JS PWA dashboard served via GitHub Pages with a Gist as source-of-truth, a self-looping GitHub Actions dispatcher firing Python worker scripts against the DokoKin API, Cloudflare Workers for heartbeats and PAT-hiding proxy, a finance JPY-forecast sub-project, and reusable Copilot skill scripts.

tour.title: Project Intent & Knowledge Base
tour.description: Start here to understand what this system actually is: a zero-backend automation platform for FJP/DokoKin kintai (attendance). KNOWLEDGE.md is the central repository map, commit conventions, and hard-won gotchas catalog, while copilot-instructions.md is the 129-section architecture bible covering the scheduled-dispatch internals, CloudSync, biometric unlock, and the AI Coach. Read these before any code — the stale README will mislead you, but these two documents are the true source of project intent.

tour.title: The PWA Dashboard Entry
tour.description: The user-facing surface is a vanilla-JS Progressive Web App served via GitHub Pages. docs/index.html is the main dashboard page, embedding inline theming, attendance actions, and an AI Coach UI. The manifest.json declares the installable PWA metadata (icons, display mode, start URL) that lets the dashboard behave like a native app on phones — the primary device for checking in and out.
tour.languageLesson: A PWA manifest plus a service worker (seen in Step 9) are the two ingredients that make a web page installable and offline-capable. The manifest's 'display: standalone' removes browser chrome so the app feels native.

tour.title: Application Shell & Auth Vault
tour.description: app.js is the heart of the frontend: it implements the encrypted session vault, tab navigation, an authenticated GitHub API fetch layer with retry/ETag caching, auto-lock, theming, and biometric unlock bootstrap. auth.js is the lightweight gatekeeper that reads the GitHub PAT from sessionStorage and redirects unauthenticated users to login. Because there is no backend, the browser itself holds credentials and talks directly to the GitHub API — making this vault the project's security boundary.
tour.languageLesson: ETag-based conditional requests (If-None-Match) let the app cache GitHub API responses and avoid burning rate limit on unchanged data — essential when the browser is the only client.

tour.title: The Schedule Data Model
tour.description: Building on the authenticated fetch layer from Step 3, schedule.js renders recurring check-in/checkout scheduling, the calendar/queue views, conflict detection, and even client-side dispatch of overdue jobs. scheduled-runs.json shows the schema for these scheduled runs — note that this file is only a seed/sample; the real runtime source of truth lives in a GitHub Gist. This Gist-as-database pattern is what makes the whole system backend-free.

tour.title: The Dispatcher Heart
tour.description: This is the core of the entire system. scheduled-dispatch.yml is a self-looping GitHub Actions workflow running on staggered 15-minute crons that reads the Gist schedule and fires due jobs (check-in/out, fetches, reports). dispatcher_lease.py implements a Gist-backed distributed lease so that — despite overlapping cron runs — only one dispatcher acts at a time. Together they turn free GitHub Actions minutes into a reliable always-on scheduler with no server.
tour.languageLesson: A distributed lease (lock with a heartbeat/expiry) prevents race conditions when multiple workers wake at once. Storing it in a Gist with compare-and-swap writes gives mutual exclusion without any database.

tour.title: Worker Workflows → DokoKin API
tour.description: When the dispatcher decides a job is due, it triggers worker workflows. auto-checkin.yml and auto-checkout.yml each invoke gh_checkin.py, which refreshes Azure/DokoKin tokens, matches the scheduled action, and performs the actual dakoku (punch) call against the DokoKin attendance API — sending a LINE notification on failure. This is where the abstract schedule finally becomes a real attendance record.

tour.title: Resilience: Heartbeat & Watchdog
tour.description: GitHub Actions cron timing is best-effort, so the system layers in self-healing. heartbeat.yml compensates for late or skipped dispatcher crons, while dispatcher-watchdog.yml runs on staggered crons to detect and recover hung dispatcher runs by cross-checking the lease from Step 5. These workflows are why the attendance automation keeps firing on time even when the underlying scheduler is unreliable.

tour.title: Cloudflare Workers: External Heartbeats & PAT Proxy
tour.description: Two small Cloudflare Workers harden the system from the edge. cloudflare-worker/heartbeat.js fires cron-based repository_dispatch events into GitHub as an independent external nudge for the dispatcher, while worker/src/index.js is a security proxy that injects a server-held PAT into browser→GitHub API calls behind strict origin/route/method allowlists. fare.js is a companion worker serving Suica fare data. These give the browser app capabilities it cannot safely do alone.
tour.languageLesson: A PAT-hiding proxy keeps secrets off the client: the browser sends an origin-checked request, and the edge worker attaches the real token server-side, so a token never ships in JavaScript.

tour.title: Offline & Cloud Sync
tour.description: sw.js is the service worker that makes the dashboard installable and resilient: stale-while-revalidate for the app shell, cache-first for static assets, and network-first for API calls with freshness stamping. cloud-sync.js complements it by syncing settings and state through the Gist, so the same configuration follows the user across devices — the offline and multi-device backbone of the PWA.
tour.languageLesson: Service worker caching strategies trade freshness vs speed: 'stale-while-revalidate' shows cached content instantly then updates in the background, ideal for an app shell that changes rarely.

tour.title: OT Planner & AI Coach
tour.description: On top of attendance, the app adds overtime intelligence. ot-planner.js manages OT request CRUD with DokoKin sync, conflict auto-fix, income/budget estimation, and an optimizer, while ot-salary.js handles the salary math behind it. ai-agent.js is the OT Coach: it streams responses from GitHub Models in an OpenAI-compatible, read-only tool-calling loop with client-side rate limiting, giving users conversational guidance grounded in their own data.

tour.title: Finance Forecast Sub-Project
tour.description: finance/ is a self-contained Python JPY-forecast pipeline, triggered by its own workflow. main.py orchestrates crawlers, deduplication, Gemini analysis, factor scoring, outcome verification, and email reporting; config.py centralizes its settings; and crawlers/base.py is the shared base class all source crawlers inherit. It demonstrates the repo's pattern of bolting independent automation sub-systems onto the same GitHub Actions + Gist substrate.
tour.languageLesson: Python abstract base classes let every crawler share a common interface (e.g. a fetch() contract) while customizing per-source logic — the Template Method pattern applied to data ingestion.

tour.title: Reusable Copilot Skills
tour.description: Finally, the .github/skills/ directory packages standalone, reusable automation as Copilot skills. The dokokin-auto-checkin skill's auto_checkin.py performs an Azure AD login and direct DokoKin punch, configured by checkin_config.json and documented by its SKILL.md. These skills let the same attendance and Suica/finance capabilities be invoked interactively, closing the loop between the automated workflows and on-demand human use.
